keywords: functional connectivity pathways support fear avoidance processes
topics.title: Brain Mechanisms of Chronic Pain
topics.description: This cluster centers on chronic pain conditions and their neural correlates, including pain perception, central sensitization, and brain network differences. It also highlights links between pain, avoidance processes, and imaging-based markers across specific pain disorders.

keywords: functional mapping of cerebellothalamic networks after focused ultrasound
topics.title: Focused Ultrasound for Essential Tremor Networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on focused ultrasound thalamotomy for essential tremor and the network-level mechanisms underlying treatment response. It emphasizes cerebellothalamic connectivity, structural and functional changes, and connectivity fingerprints associated with durable outcomes.

topics.title: Neuroimaging of Suicide Risk and Depression
topics.description: This cluster focuses on brain imaging findings related to suicide risk, suicidal ideation, and suicide attempts across depressive and bipolar disorders. It highlights network connectivity, amygdala connectivity, hippocampal volumes, and white matter microstructure alterations in psychiatric populations.

keywords: constrained dynamic functional connectivity predicts surgical outcome
eeg base connectome analysis and machine learning predict surgical outcome in pediatric epilepsy
topics.title: Epilepsy Localization and Surgical Outcome
topics.description: This cluster centers on identifying epileptogenic and seizure onset zones in epilepsy, especially temporal lobe and pediatric cases. It also includes connectome- and EEG-based approaches for prediction and localization related to surgical outcomes.

topics.title: Sleep Dynamics, Aging, and Glymphatic Function
topics.description: This cluster centers on human sleep, especially NREM structure, brain-state dynamics, and electrophysiological features such as K complexes and spindles. It also highlights aging, insomnia-related memory effects, and sleep-linked glymphatic/CSF-hemodynamic processes.

topics.title: Network Reorganization in Brain Tumor Surgery
topics.description: This cluster centers on how glioma and other brain tumors alter structural and functional brain networks, and how surgery-related lesions shape remapping and compensatory reorganization. It also highlights links between connectivity changes and cognitive impairment, postoperative deficits, and recovery prediction.

keywords: post mortem human midbrain t2*w mri
cortical microarchitecture along major vascular territories
topics.title: High-Resolution MRI of Cortical Vasculature
topics.description: The cluster centers on high-resolution MRI approaches for mapping and quantifying human brain vasculature and related cortical microarchitecture. It emphasizes vessel segmentation, vascular morphology, and analyses across major vascular territories.

keywords: sectoral retinal nerve fibre layer thickness
brain structural correlates of sectoral retinal nerve fibre layer thickness
topics.title: Glaucoma-related visual and brain structure
topics.description: This cluster centers on glaucoma and its structural consequences across the optic nerve, retinal nerve fiber layer, and visual cortex. It also includes normative and data-driven approaches to link ocular damage with brain structural correlates.

keywords: lsd reshapes cortical dynamics through peak frequency shifts
topics.title: Psychedelic Effects on Brain Network Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on how psychedelics such as psilocybin and LSD alter cortical organization, connectivity, and resting-state brain dynamics. The abstracts emphasize network reorganization, entropy, modularity, and spatiotemporal measures of neural activity.

keywords: cerebellar thalamo cortical triple network alteration
topics.title: Cerebellar Connectivity and Lifespan Development
topics.description: This cluster focuses on cerebellar structure and functional connectivity across development, aging, and neurodevelopmental conditions. It highlights cortical-cerebellar network alterations, cerebellar morphology, and lifespan-related trajectories that may relate to cognitive and behavioral vulnerability.

topics.title: Neurometabolic Connectivity in Human Brain Networks
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how metabolic and mitochondrial features shape brain network organization, especially through MRSI-based connectomics and gradient analyses. It emphasizes neurometabolic signatures, cortical metabolic connectivity, and their links to brain disorders and neurodegeneration.

topics.title: Consciousness and Anesthesia Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on how propofol and related anesthesia alter consciousness, anesthesia depth, and brain dynamics measured with EEG and functional connectivity. It emphasizes thalamocortical mechanisms, brain network models, and complexity or entropy-based analyses of resting-state activity.

topics.title: Thalamocortical Development and Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on the organization, development, and functional roles of thalamic and thalamocortical circuits, especially in the developing brain. It also links structural and functional mapping approaches to cognitive function, sensory processing, and subcortical pathways.

topics.title: Brain Injury Connectivity and Symptom Networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on how traumatic brain injury and related injuries alter structural and functional brain connectivity across cortical and limbic networks. It also links multimodal neuroimaging and network mapping approaches to cognitive change and symptom markers.

keywords: a tool for transparent and standardized assessment of multisite harmonization in neuroimaging
bayesian non parametric serial mri harmonization
topics.title: Multisite Neuroimaging Data Harmonization Methods
topics.description: This cluster focuses on correcting site, batch, and harmonization-related effects in neuroimaging data across multi-site studies. It emphasizes standardized assessment tools, joint harmonization frameworks, and method comparison for improving harmonisation efficacy.

keywords: microstructrure of neuromodulatory nuclei predict cognitive decline
topics.title: Prodromal REM Sleep Disorder and Cognitive Decline
topics.description: This cluster centers on isolated REM sleep behavior disorder as a prodromal marker of Lewy body disease and phenoconversion risk. It emphasizes longitudinal neuromodulatory changes and their links to executive and cognitive decline.

keywords: mri base spinal cord morphology link
personalized spinal cord dynamic functional connectivity
potential of personalized spinal cord dynamic functional connectivity across regions and pathology
somatotopic homunculus profiles of motor cortex
topics.title: Spinal Cord and Motor Cortex Reorganization
topics.description: These studies examine structural and functional alterations in the human cervical spinal cord and motor cortex, especially in degenerative cervical myelopathy. They highlight corticospinal and sensory-motor pathway changes, somatotopic organization, and personalized connectivity across regions and pathology.

keywords: early parietofrontal connectivity predicts visuomotor recovery after stroke
topics.title: Stroke Lesions and Recovery Prediction
topics.description: This cluster centers on how stroke lesions and network disruptions relate to motor, cognitive, and language recovery outcomes. It emphasizes predictive markers from connectivity, lesion mapping, and longitudinal follow-up in acute to chronic stroke.

topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Fatigue Across Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on fatigue and cognitive fatigue in conditions such as multiple sclerosis, long covid, and CFS. It emphasizes neural correlates and network-level alterations identified through multimodal assessment, functional MRI, and EEG-based biomarkers.

keywords: spatial trends in brain maps evidence and recommendation
topics.title: Spatial Organization and Analysis of Brain Maps
topics.description: This cluster centers on spatial structure in brain maps, including multimodal parcellation, map-to-map associations, and self-similar organization across cortex and human brain data. It also includes statistical and geometric methods such as spherical projection, spin tests, and spatial interpolation for evaluating spatial trends and significance.

topics.title: Neural Dynamics in Dyslexia and Reading Disability
topics.description: This cluster focuses on neural mechanisms and brain-network markers associated with dyslexia and broader reading disability, including prediction, variability, and noise-based hypotheses. It also includes related work on sensory-motor processing, entrainment, and structural or functional asymmetries in adult and autistic individuals.

keywords: music and emotion in functional connectivity
cortico subcortical mechanisms of dynamic music reward processing
topics.title: Neural Bases of Music Perception and Reward
topics.description: This cluster centers on how music is encoded, remembered, and emotionally processed in the brain, with emphasis on reward, melody, and temporal-cortical mechanisms. It also includes work on music training and participation as they relate to neural specialization and skill retention.

topics.title: Language processing and lateralization in aphasia
topics.description: This cluster centers on language and speech processing, especially aphasia, reading, and comprehension, with emphasis on multilingual and bilingual contexts. It also highlights brain fMRI studies of language networks, lateralization, and key regions and pathways such as Broca area and the arcuate fasciculus.

topics.title: Neural Signatures of Tinnitus Perception
topics.description: This cluster centers on the neural and structural correlates of tinnitus, including auditory and prefrontal circuitry, neurotransmitter markers, and gray matter alterations. It also highlights differences in tinnitus subtype, loudness, suppression, and shared brain signatures across patients.

keywords: evolution of the auditory cortex duplication of heschl 's gyrus in great apes
topics.title: Evolution of Auditory and Hemispheric Brain Organization
topics.description: This cluster centers on comparative neuroscience of great apes and humans, focusing on the evolution of the auditory cortex, language-related organization, and hemispheric specialization. It also examines how white matter connectivity and interhemispheric differences relate to brain function across species.

keywords: high fidelity infant brain mri segmentation
topics.title: Automated Fetal and Infant Brain MRI Segmentation
topics.description: This cluster centers on automated and open-source pipelines for fetal and infant brain MRI analysis, including segmentation, surface reconstruction, and template or atlas construction. It also highlights comparisons among pipelines and age-conditioned approaches for developmental brain imaging.

keywords: decode voice identity in auditory working memory
topics.title: Neural Bases of Speech and Voice Processing
topics.description: This cluster centers on how the brain supports speech perception, voice identity, and auditory working memory, with attention to individual differences and listening effort. It also includes studies using imaging and electrophysiological methods to localize language-related and cortical processing mechanisms.

keywords: semantic information in environmental sound categories
topics.title: Auditory Cortex Encoding and Semantic Learning
topics.description: This cluster centers on how the auditory cortex and related networks encode natural sounds, semantic information, and prediction violations. It also spans developmental and hierarchical organization in human auditory processing, including MEG, fMRI, and iEEG findings.

topics.title: Appetite Disorders and Brain Network Alterations
topics.description: This cluster centers on eating and appetite-related disorders and their associated brain network alterations, especially hypothalamic, striatal, and resting-state connectivity changes. It also includes biomarker-oriented work linking functional and structural brain measures to obesity, anorexia, binge eating, and Prader-Willi syndrome.

topics.title: Open Neuroimaging Infrastructure and Workflows
topics.description: This cluster centers on open, collaborative neuroimaging infrastructure for data organization, tool integration, and scalable analysis. It emphasizes BIDS-based workflows, containerization, and clinical or consortium-oriented platforms such as cbrain and Clinica.

keywords: medial prefrontal glutamate and default mode network

topics.title: Neurodevelopmental Correlates of ADHD in Youth
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain structure, connectivity, and functional characteristics associated with ADHD and related symptom dimensions in children and youth. It emphasizes developmental trajectories, regional volume responses, and links to glutamate, default mode network, and social responsiveness traits.

keywords: abnormal functional hierarchy development during childhood in autism
topics.title: Autism Subtypes and Functional Brain Alterations
topics.description: This cluster focuses on autism spectrum disorder, emphasizing symptom heterogeneity, clinically distinct subgroups, and developmental variation across childhood and adulthood. It also highlights functional brain connectivity and hierarchy alterations associated with autism.

topics.title: Brain structure and cognition in diabetes
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how type 2 diabetes and prediabetes relate to brain structure, white matter integrity, glucose metabolism, and cognitive outcomes across adulthood and youth onset cases. It also includes the modifying effects of physical exercise and related metabolic factors.

keywords: brain sensitivity across the menstrual cycle an eeg study

topics.title: Menopause, Hormones, and Brain Function
topics.description: This cluster centers on how menstrual and menopausal hormonal changes relate to brain function, connectivity, and memory across midlife and aging. It highlights neuroendocrine influences on hippocampal structure, neural activity, and cognitive change.

topics.title: Mindfulness Training and Brain Network Coupling
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how mindfulness and related meditation practices affect brain functionality and functional brain networks, especially resting-state coupling and connectivity. It also links meditative practice to cognitive gains and network properties such as hub organization and somatomotor connectivity.

keywords: sex stratified brain aging longitudinal sleep relate vulnerability
topics.title: Sex Differences in Brain Aging Networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on how brain aging and functional connectivity differ across sexes and across the lifespan. It also includes network changes relevant to early Alzheimer disease and hippocampal organization.

brain network modularity predict training relate improvements

topics.title: Math Learning and Dyscalculia Development
topics.description: This cluster focuses on developmental math difficulties and how math performance changes with training across childhood and adolescence. It also includes neural network reorganization and brain specialization associated with learning, working memory, and arithmetic retrieval.

keywords: infant laminar cortical myelination associated with behavior and environment
topics.title: Infant Cortical Myelination and Development
topics.description: This cluster centers on laminar and intracortical myelin development in infants and early life, with emphasis on environmental and behavioral associations. It also includes longitudinal and MRI-based approaches for measuring myelin maturation and related brain changes.

keywords: neuroanatomical subtypes of callous unemotional traits
topics.title: Neurodevelopmental Subtypes in Youth Psychopathology
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain morphology and neurodevelopmental variation in youth, especially in relation to psychosis risk, callous unemotional traits, and other transdiagnostic mental dimensions. Studies emphasize heterogeneous pathways, cortical and subcortical brain change, and neuroanatomical subtypes across community and high-risk samples.

keywords: hierarchical organization of sex differences in functional connectivity during youth
contextual functional connectomics of sex differences in cognition
topics.title: Sex Differences in Adolescent Brain Development
topics.description: This cluster centers on how sex differences shape adolescent neurodevelopment, brain structure, and functional connectivity. It also includes hormonal influences, prenatal factors, and related cognitive or gender-diversity associations across development.

keywords: prenatal maternal mood modulates longitudinal trajectories of brain development
prenatal mental health early brain structure shape infant temperament
maternal distress × cortico limbic connectivity predicts
topics.title: Perinatal Mental Health and Infant Brain Development
topics.description: This cluster centers on how prenatal and perinatal maternal mood and distress relate to early brain development, connectivity, and later infant or child outcomes. It highlights associations with structural and functional neural features, including risk for postpartum depression and early autism-related trajectories.

topics.title: Adolescent Brain Development and Puberty
topics.description: This cluster centers on normative brain development during adolescence, with emphasis on pubertal maturation, pubertal tempo, and puberty effects. It also includes studies of functional cortical organization, white matter network reorganisation, and longitudinal neuroimaging analyses across age.

keywords: cross population variation in brain structural development during
topics.title: Population Brain Development Across Early Life
topics.description: This cluster centers on normative and population-specific patterns of brain structural development from the neonatal period through adolescence and the first decades of life. It emphasizes variation by gestational age, birthweight, and preterm birth, with MRI-based cohort and modeling approaches.

topics.title: White Matter Development Across Early Life
topics.description: This cluster centers on developmental changes in white matter across infancy, childhood, and youth, with emphasis on microstructural maturation and longitudinal trajectories. It also includes studies linking white matter development to cognitive outcomes, preterm birth, and early-life risk factors.

topics.title: Dynamic Brain Connectivity in Alzheimer’s Disease
topics.description: This cluster centers on dynamic and resting-state brain connectivity alterations in Alzheimer’s disease and mild cognitive impairment, including default mode network changes and network topology. It also highlights heterogeneity, progression, and related analytic frameworks for identifying altered brain states.

keywords: brainstem cortex ans effective connectivity contributes
topics.title: Parkinsonian Connectivity and Symptom Biomarkers
topics.description: This cluster centers on Parkinson disease and related motor and non-motor symptoms, especially freezing of gait, hallucinations, and broader gait impairment. The abstracts emphasize functional and structural biomarkers across brainstem, cortical, metabolic, and EEG connectivity measures.

keywords: spatiotemporal pattern of individual functional variability in youth
topics.title: Developmental Reorganization of Functional Brain Networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on how brain networks and functional connectivity change across development, especially during childhood and adolescence. It emphasizes variability, network reorganization, and dynamic patterns observed in both resting-state and naturalistic fMRI data.

topics.title: Infant Brain Network Development in Preterm Birth
topics.description: This cluster centers on how early brain networks develop in newborns and preterm infants, with emphasis on functional connectivity, resting-state organization, and developmental change. It also highlights nonlinear and population-level transformations in network maturation during early life.

topics.title: Iron and Neuromelanin in Parkinson Disease
topics.description: This cluster centers on Parkinson disease, emphasizing iron accumulation, substantia nigra degeneration, and distinct brain iron patterns across basal ganglia and cortex. It also includes MRI-based characterization of subtypes, microstructure alteration, and spatiotemporal trajectories in early and subtype-specific disease.

topics.title: Neural and Behavioral Mechanisms of Addiction
topics.description: This cluster centers on substance use disorders across alcohol, cannabis, and methamphetamine, with emphasis on craving, cue reactivity, reward, and inhibitory control. It also highlights adolescent brain development and functional connectivity changes related to substance exposure and abstinence.

topics.title: Cortical Folding and Development
topics.description: This cluster centers on how cortical geometry and folding emerge during brain development, including neonatal and ferret models. It also includes computational and normative approaches for quantifying folding, curvature, and shape-related developmental patterns.

keywords: atlas guided region wise shapley value
topics.title: Explainable Deep Learning for Brain MRI Diagnosis
topics.description: This cluster centers on deep learning and explainable AI methods for classifying neurodegenerative disease from brain MRI, especially structural and altered MRI patterns. It emphasizes model performance, region-wise attribution, and diagnosis of dementia, Alzheimer disease, and multiple system atrophy.

topics.title: Amyloid and Tau in Alzheimer Disease Progression
topics.description: This cluster centers on Alzheimer disease biology, especially amyloid and tau pathology, and how these relate to disease progression and heterogeneity. It also includes biomarker and PET-based approaches for understanding preclinical disease and predicting cognitive decline.

keywords: local brain aging in cognitively normal
partial volume effects shape hemispheric asymmetry
topics.title: Local Brain Aging and White Matter Change
topics.description: This cluster centers on age-related structural change in cognitively normal adults, with emphasis on local brain aging, corpus callosum microstructure trajectories, and white matter alterations. It also highlights how tractography and partial volume effects shape observed hemispheric asymmetry and cohort-level aging patterns.

topics.title: Motor Control, Observation, and Body Representation
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how primary motor cortex and related networks support movement, imagery, grasping, and action observation. It also includes studies of body representation, kinesthesia, and intervention effects such as action observation training and epidural spinal stimulation.

topics.title: Cytoarchitectonic Brain Atlases and Parcellation
topics.description: This cluster centers on constructing and refining cytoarchitectonic brain atlases and parcellation frameworks across humans and primates. It emphasizes coordinate systems, histological reconstruction, and surface-based mapping for comparing cortical organization across species.

keywords: traumatic brain injury and post traumatic
topics.title: PTSD Network and Trauma Biomarker Studies
topics.description: This cluster centers on post traumatic stress disorder across trauma-exposed groups, including combat, intimate partner violence, and traumatic brain injury. Studies emphasize network connectivity, structural-functional signatures, and prediction of PTSD-related outcomes.

keywords: the transferability of foundation models to cortical folding
topics.title: Cross-Species Cerebellar Mapping and Segmentation
topics.description: This cluster centers on MRI-based cerebellar analysis across species, including segmentation, vermis morphology, and atlas construction in human, macaque, and marmoset data. It also links to evaluating transferability of foundation and unsupervised representation learning approaches, especially for cortical folding.

keywords: deep learning base brain age prediction
accurate voxel wise brain age prediction
convolutional neural network for brain age prediction
functional connectivity base brain age gap
topics.title: Brain Age Prediction and Neuroimaging
topics.description: This cluster focuses on estimating and mapping brain age from MRI and related neuroimaging data, often using deep learning and normative reference models. It also addresses generalization across populations, clinical interpretation, and links between brain age measures and cognitive or disease-related outcomes.

keywords: dimensions of psychiatry for clinical prediction
topics.title: Machine Learning for Psychiatric Imaging Classification
topics.description: This cluster centers on machine learning approaches for psychiatric diagnosis and clinical prediction using MRI and resting-state fMRI data. It emphasizes harmonization, multimodal imaging, and classification across disorders such as schizophrenia, bipolar disorder, and depression.

keywords: clinical translation of eeg dementia predictors
topics.title: Normative EEG Models for Dementia
topics.description: This cluster centers on normative EEG modeling and harmonisation across multi-site data to support dementia-related prediction and clinical translation. It spans Alzheimer disease and related disorders, emphasizing heterogeneity, individual differences, and comparison against patient groups.

topics.title: Aging Effects on Cognitive Electrophysiology
topics.description: This cluster centers on how aging and lifespan-related change affect cognitive function and brain electrophysiology. It includes resting-state and event-related measures such as EEG, MEG spectral power, and P300, with emphasis on temporal dynamics and age-dependent markers.

keywords: cognitive task evoke brain body interactions
cardiac pulsatility generates structured network specific bold dynamics
explore brainstem autonomic and cardiorespiratory signal interaction
topics.title: Brain-Body Cardiorespiratory Interaction Networks
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how cardiac, respiratory, and interoceptive signals interact with brain activity across tasks and rest. It emphasizes brain-heart coupling, insular and autonomic pathways, and related clinical or transdiagnostic phenomena such as breathlessness and functional dyspepsia.

keywords: the motion sick brain spectral changes
topics.title: Motion Sickness Brain Dynamics and EEG
topics.description: This cluster centers on neural and spectral changes associated with motion sickness, including susceptibility and brain responses during rotating chair experiments. It also includes EEG-based approaches, wearable EEG, and motor-cognitive coupling analyses relevant to understanding and potentially modulating these effects.

topics.title: Diffusion-Based Brain Microstructure and Tractography
topics.description: This cluster centers on diffusion MRI and related imaging methods for estimating brain microstructure, fiber architecture, and tractography. It also includes advanced ex vivo and microscopy-based approaches for validating connectivity and cortical organization.

topics.title: White Matter Aging and Vascular Risk
topics.description: This cluster centers on white matter hyperintensities and related white matter changes in aging and neurodegenerative disease. It emphasizes vascular risk, cerebrovascular function, and links to cognitive outcomes and dementia risk.

keywords: multimodal imaging signatures of cognitive decline
topics.title: Neuroimaging Signatures of Cognitive Decline
topics.description: This cluster centers on imaging-based phenotypes and volumetric changes associated with cognitive decline across conditions such as subjective cognitive decline, small vessel disease, Parkinson disease, and SARS-CoV-2 associated dementia. The abstracts emphasize multimodal and voxel-based neuroimaging approaches to characterize early-stage brain changes.

keywords: hypoperfusion loadings for outcome prediction in acute ischemic stroke

robust ensemble learning for practical ischemic stroke lesion segmentation
deep learning models of acute stroke severity
topics.title: Deep Learning for Acute Stroke MRI Analysis
topics.description: This cluster centers on MRI-based analysis of acute ischemic stroke, including diagnosis, lesion segmentation, severity assessment, and functional outcome prediction. It also includes synthetic perfusion map generation and robust ensemble methods for practical stroke imaging workflows.

topics.title: AI-Driven Low-Field MRI Reconstruction
topics.description: This cluster centers on deep learning and diffusion-model methods for reconstructing, accelerating, and enhancing low-field and ultra-low-field MRI. It also includes registration, super-resolution, and automated analysis approaches for diffusion and contrast-enhanced brain imaging.

topics.title: Deep Learning for Neuroimage Segmentation
topics.description: This cluster centers on automated segmentation methods for brain MRI and microscopy data, especially using deep learning pipelines and state-of-the-art segmentation techniques. It includes applications to synaptic vesicles, axon bundles, medial temporal lobe subregions, corpus callosum morphometry, and human LGN/amygdala analysis.

topics.title: Longitudinal MRI Segmentation Robustness
topics.description: This cluster focuses on the robustness and reproducibility of brain MRI segmentation across longitudinal and test-retest settings. It emphasizes benchmarking, stability of volumetric estimates, and methods for estimating changes in imaging-derived phenotypes across datasets and scanners.

topics.title: MRI Microstructure in Alzheimer Continuum
topics.description: This cluster focuses on diffusion MRI and related signal-processing approaches for detecting microstructural alteration across the Alzheimer disease continuum, including AD+late and related risk states. It emphasizes biomarkers that go beyond cortical atrophy to map cellular microstructure and differentiate disease stages in vivo.

topics.title: Normative Brain Imaging in Multiple Sclerosis
topics.description: This cluster focuses on multiple sclerosis-related brain abnormalities, including white matter lesions, thalamic atrophy, and brain volume changes, and their links to disability, cognitive status, and disease severity. It also emphasizes normative modelling and prediction for diagnosis and prognosis in MS.

keywords: human brain dynamics change across the lifespan
topics.title: Aging and Brain Network Organization Across Lifespan
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how aging and the adult lifespan relate to changes in brain dynamics, functional connectivity, and network organization. It also emphasizes links between these neural changes and cognitive variability, especially executive function in healthy aging.

keywords: leisure relate cognitive reserve modulates resilience

topics.title: Aging, cognition, and brain resilience
topics.description: This cluster centers on how aging-related factors shape cognitive performance and cognitive function across midlife and late adulthood. It links brain structure, biological aging markers, and lifestyle or reserve-related influences on resilience in healthy older adults.

keywords: brain structural and functional moderation of environment social emotion association during
topics.title: Adolescent Brain Development and Environmental Risk
topics.description: This cluster centers on how adolescent neurodevelopment relates to environmental, family, and social influences on cognition and psychopathology. It emphasizes risk and protective factors, including structural and functional brain associations and developmental moderation effects.

keywords: the protective role of memory suppression
topics.title: Childhood Trauma, Resilience, and Brain Signatures
topics.description: This cluster centers on how childhood trauma and maltreatment relate to emotion regulation, memory suppression, and psychological resilience across mental health outcomes. It also highlights neurobiological and functional connectivity markers that may explain transdiagnostic vulnerability and protection.

keywords: the brain age approach to identify psychological resilience
topics.title: Neural and Genetic Resilience in Adolescence
topics.description: This cluster centers on psychological resilience in the context of childhood and early life adversity, with attention to developmental risk, emotional distress, and adolescent outcomes. It also includes work linking resilience to neural measures and genetic influences, including brain age approaches and polygenic effects.

keywords: adaptive interplay of action observation and mentalizing networks during ambiguous social perception
topics.title: Naturalistic Social Cognition and Interbrain Coupling
topics.description: This cluster centers on how people interpret and coordinate with others in naturalistic, ambiguous, and interactive settings, including social perception, empathy, and conversation. It also spans group dynamics such as musical cooperation, creativity, and interbrain neural coupling.

keywords: the brain network underlying social participation a multimodal
neurocomputational mapping of social norm transgression

topics.title: Neural Basis of Social Behavior and Norms
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how brain lesions and network disruptions affect social cognition, social participation, and responses to social norm transgression. It combines lesion, disconnection, and multimodal structural approaches to identify neurostructural and neurocomputational correlates of social behavior change.

topics.title: OCD Youth Treatment and Brain Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on obsessive compulsive disorder in youth, with emphasis on CBT, psychotherapy, and treatment response. It also highlights brain activation, frontostriatal circuitry, and structural or functional connectivity measures linked to OCD characteristics and outcomes.

topics.title: TMS Effects on OCD Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on therapeutic brain stimulation for OCD, especially TMS and related stimulation approaches targeting ACC, SMA, and ventral capsule circuits. The abstracts emphasize clinical response and connectivity changes, including frontoparietal and CSTC network reorganization measured with resting-state fMRI.

keywords: personalized fc- e field guide precision tms fmri
topics.title: TMS Effects on Brain Networks and Neurochemistry
topics.description: This cluster centers on how TMS and related stimulation methods modulate brain activity, connectivity, and BOLD responses across cortical and network targets. It also includes studies linking stimulation effects to neurochemical measures such as GABA and to temporal dynamics of cortical responses.

topics.title: Deep Brain Stimulation and Network Modeling in Parkinson’s
topics.description: This cluster centers on Parkinson disease research linking deep brain stimulation, STN effects, and DBS-L-dopa therapy with changes in functional networks. It also includes computational and personalized brain modeling approaches for understanding and modulating human brain circuits.

topics.title: Stress-Related Brain and Clinical Effects
topics.description: This cluster centers on how stress alters brain dynamics, neural processing, and functional integration across systems such as the default mode network and cortical gradients. It also includes clinical effects and disease contexts including irritable bowel syndrome and parkinson disease.

keywords: tms coil orientation modulates motor threshold estimation and cortical silent period duration

topics.title: Optimizing TMS targeting and threshold estimation
topics.description: This cluster focuses on methods for improving transcranial magnetic stimulation targeting, motor mapping, and motor threshold estimation. It includes coil orientation, personalized montage optimization, and probabilistic or Bayesian guidance approaches for more accurate stimulation and localization.

topics.title: Transcranial Ultrasound Neuromodulation in Humans
topics.description: This cluster centers on transcranial ultrasound approaches for modulating human brain circuits, often studied with fMRI and multimodal designs. The abstracts emphasize target-specific effects, neural synchrony, and rapid neuroplastic changes across thalamic and cingulate circuitry.

topics.title: Neural and Computational Bases of Decision Making
topics.description: This cluster centers on how reward, value, and prediction error signals shape decision making, risk taking, exploration-exploitation tradeoffs, and habitual behavior. It links neural correlates in regions such as the ventromedial prefrontal cortex and human ventral tegmental area with computational modeling of choice and value computation.

keywords: homeostatic and hedonic neural correlates of food memory relate

topics.title: Neural Correlates of Food Choice and Memory
topics.description: This cluster centers on how brain activity and neural dynamics relate to food choice, food memory, and taste-related processing. It also includes work on naturalistic food viewing, multimodal imaging, and how distraction or homeostatic/hedonic factors shape food-related decisions.

keywords: erp correlates of medical source credibility
topics.title: Neural and Behavioral Responses to Misinformation
topics.description: This cluster centers on how people evaluate fabricated or manipulated information, including the effects of warnings, source credibility, and trust violations. Studies combine behavioral, eye-tracking, ERP, fMRI, and Bayesian approaches to examine belief integration, susceptibility, and cognitive engagement.

topics.title: EEG Dynamics in Cognition and Perception
topics.description: This cluster centers on EEG and related neural dynamics underlying attention, perception, memory, and time estimation. It highlights alpha-band and aperiodic activity, functional connectivity, and task-based cognitive processes across working memory, visuospatial attention, and inhibitory control.

topics.title: Heterogeneity and Biomarkers in Depression
topics.description: This cluster focuses on depression and major depressive disorder as heterogeneous conditions, emphasizing clinically distinct subtypes and symptom-linked brain differences across large multi-cohort imaging samples. It also highlights biomarker and inflammation-related approaches, including blood biomarker, metabolomic brain signature, and imaging microglia density.

keywords: large data drive multimodal power benchmarking
topics.title: fMRI Reproducibility and Power Analysis Methods
topics.description: This cluster centers on statistical methods for assessing reproducibility, effect sizes, and power in fMRI and related brain studies. It emphasizes benchmarking, multicentre analysis, and variability modeling across datasets and study designs.

keywords: online evaluation of eeg ocular artifact removal under controlled contamination
universal low cost open source device
topics.title: EEG Quality, Denoising, and Neurofeedback Tools
topics.description: This cluster centers on methods for improving EEG data quality, denoising, and artifact removal, often in online or controlled-evaluation settings. It also includes neurofeedback systems and open-source, reproducible tooling for scalable neurophysiology research.

topics.title: Aperiodic Brain Network Connectivity in Resting State
topics.description: This cluster centers on resting-state brain networks, cortical activity, and aperiodic dynamics underlying brain connectivity. It also highlights computational and causal modeling approaches used to distinguish effective and functional connectivity across neural systems.

topics.title: Hippocampal Mechanisms in Memory
topics.description: This cluster centers on hippocampal contributions to episodic and working memory, including encoding, consolidation, and network-level neural correlates. It also highlights functional organization within the hippocampus and dissociable memory circuits across memory processes.

topics.title: Neurofeedback and Self-Regulation in Brain Networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on how decoded neurofeedback and self-regulation relate to brain network organization, cortical and subcortical markers, and individual differences in adaptation. It also includes studies of neural activity, perceptual decision making, and network engagement during task performance and state transitions.

topics.title: Genetic Architecture of Brain Structure and Cognition
topics.description: This cluster centers on how genetic variation shapes brain structure, connectivity, and cognition across development and neuropsychiatric contexts. It highlights genome-wide and multimodal analyses linking heritable brain traits to psychiatric risk and cognitive outcomes.

keywords: individualized parcellation reveal dorsal attention network specificity
topics.title: Dynamic Connectivity and Individual Attention
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how dynamic and individualized fMRI-based connectivity patterns relate to attention and broader cognitive performance. The studies emphasize brain-state dynamics, personalized parcellation, and predictive models of cognitive function.

topics.title: Neurostimulation and Treatment Response in Depression
topics.description: This cluster centers on depression, especially treatment resistant depression and major depressive disorder, with emphasis on response to electroconvulsive therapy, stanford neuromodulation therapy, and related antidepressant approaches. It also highlights brain network and neural dynamics markers used to predict or explain clinical improvement.

keywords: brain signal variability profiles associate with antidepressant treatment response
topics.title: Depression Connectivity and Treatment Response
topics.description: This cluster focuses on depression-related disorders and how brain connectivity, functional topology, and dynamic resting-state patterns relate to symptom severity and treatment outcomes. It includes predictors of response to antidepressants, electroconvulsive therapy, CBT, and other interventions.

keywords: the neural underpinnings of social threat
topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Fear and Threat
topics.description: This cluster centers on how fear, threat, and anxiety are represented and regulated in the brain, including conditioning, extinction, and social threat processing. It highlights amygdala-related responses, autonomic involvement, and broader neural underpinnings of defensive behavior.

keywords: neurochemical scaffolding of evolutionarily conserved spatiotemporal dynamics
topics.title: Neurochemical Organization of Brain Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how neurochemical receptor and transporter architecture supports conserved spatiotemporal dynamics across human and macaque brains. It also considers regional organization, functional connectivity, and inter-individual variability in these patterns.

topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Emotion Regulation
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how emotion regulation is implemented in the brain, especially under conditions of emotional interference, stress, and anxiety. It includes studies of cognitive reappraisal, amygdala regulation, and neural correlates measured with fMRI and fNIRS.

topics.title: Neural Dynamics of Emotion and Affective Traits
topics.description: This cluster centers on how the brain encodes and processes emotion, valence, arousal, and facial affective cues. It also highlights individual differences and neural signatures linked to alexithymia, autistic traits, and dynamic affective transitions.

keywords: functional connectivity and genetic correlates of dimensional mood variation
topics.title: Transdiagnostic Connectivity in Psychiatric Disorders
topics.description: This cluster focuses on transdiagnostic brain connectivity and structural alterations across psychiatric disorders, with emphasis on shared and disorder-specific patterns. It also links functional and structural connectomic signatures to genetic correlates and dimensional mood variation.

topics.title: Schizophrenia Heterogeneity and Biomarker Subtypes
topics.description: This cluster centers on schizophrenia and psychosis-spectrum heterogeneity, including subtype identification, symptom phenotypes, and neurobiological markers. It also highlights links between distinct neurofunctional and molecular signatures and treatment response.

keywords: multimodal neuroimaging simultaneous integration of eeg
topics.title: Multimodal Neuroimaging Integration and Denoising
topics.description: This cluster centers on simultaneous and multimodal neuroimaging, especially EEG, MEG, and fMRI integration. It also emphasizes artifact reduction, denoising, and source modeling methods for improving data quality in challenging acquisition environments.

keywords: group fmri revisited computationally tractable bayesian hierarchical model
multivariate bayesian inversion for trial wise decoding
spatial neural modeling of hemodynamic response functions
topics.title: Bayesian Modeling of Individual Brain Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on Bayesian and spatial neural modeling approaches for fMRI and MEG analysis, with emphasis on individualized brain dynamics and hemodynamic response functions. The abstracts highlight data-driven integration, trial-wise decoding, and interpretable hierarchical models for linking task-based and resting-state signals.

keywords: time vary scale free brain dynamic
topics.title: Naturalistic Temporal Dynamics in Neural Representation
topics.description: This cluster centers on how brain activity and neural representations evolve over time during naturalistic stimulation and behavior. It emphasizes temporal structure, stable and recurring neural states, and how information flow supports perception-action integration.

topics.title: Persistent Homology in Connectome Network Analysis
topics.description: This cluster centers on network-based analyses of human brain connectivity, especially personalized and probabilistic connectome inference from resting-state and structural data. It also includes graph-theoretic and topological methods for comparing functional topology, structural connectomes, and network-level changes across conditions.

keywords: dynamic network construction graph attention decoding
scalable and accurate kernel methods for dense connectome analysis
topics.title: Multimodal Graph Methods for Brain Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on graph-based and multimodal methods for analyzing brain connectivity, including dense connectome analysis, representation learning, and interpretable neural network approaches. It also includes optimal transport, linked-dataset decomposition, and dynamic network construction for connectivity modeling.

keywords: consistent scalable brain effective connectivity estimation
latent diffusion modeling of dynamic functional brain networks
topics.title: Dynamic Brain Connectivity Modeling
topics.description: This cluster focuses on estimating and modeling dynamic functional and effective connectivity from fMRI, often using generative, spectral, or diffusion-based approaches. A recurring goal is robust network recovery under limited temporal data and scalable inference.

topics.title: Brain Connectivity and Dynamic State Transitions
topics.description: This cluster centers on how structural and functional connectivity interact to shape brain communication, network efficiency, and directed information flow. It also emphasizes dynamic brain states, including resting-state organization and consciousness-related transitions.

topics.title: Glucose-Driven Neurometabolic Imaging
topics.description: Studies using metabolic and fMRI methods to examine how glucose manipulation alters brain metabolism and related responses. The cluster also touches on glymphatic changes and uncoupling between flow and metabolism.

keywords: functional mri compatible cortical wide field fluorescent
topics.title: Awake Mouse Imaging of Neurovascular Signals
topics.description: This cluster focuses on multimodal imaging in awake mouse to relate neuronal and astrocytic activity to bold signal and hemodynamic responses. It emphasizes physiological mechanisms underlying functional mri and optical imaging signals across hemispheres and vasculature.

keywords: bold asl base cerebrovascular reactivity mapping
topics.title: Cerebrovascular Reactivity in Brain Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on measuring cerebrovascular reactivity and related baseline perfusion physiology using ASL and BOLD-based approaches. The abstracts also link these measures to clinical populations including traumatic brain injury, Alzheimer disease, glioma, and white matter effects.

topics.title: Precision and Reliability in Resting-State fMRI
topics.description: This cluster centers on improving the reliability and precision of resting-state fMRI and functional connectivity measurements. It emphasizes preprocessing, denoising, motion handling, and quality metrics for robust test-retest evaluation.

topics.title: Functional Connectome Fingerprinting and Individual Variability
topics.description: This cluster centers on identifying individuals from connectome-based fingerprints and evaluating how stable, reliable, and reproducible these fingerprints are across datasets and contexts. It also links connectome idiosyncrasy to behavioral relevance and brain-behavior correlations.

keywords: dual acquisition multi echo calibrate fmri
robust multi contrast rest state analysis
topics.title: Multi-echo fMRI Denoising and Processing
topics.description: This cluster centers on multi-echo fMRI workflows for denoising, preprocessing, and analysis, especially ICA-based approaches and tools such as tedana and MRIQC. The abstracts emphasize reliable extraction of signal across echoes and robust processing for downstream biomarker and resting-state applications.

keywords: optimize automatic interactive b0 shim procedure
topics.title: Ultra-High-Field FMRI Methods and Applications
topics.description: This cluster centers on high-field and ultra-high-field fMRI, especially 7 T acquisition, distortion correction, and shimming strategies. It also includes functional applications such as motor network and resting-state connectivity mapping.

keywords: deep neural network representational alignment predict behavioral performance
topics.title: fMRI Representations and Alignment
topics.description: This cluster centers on how human fMRI and neural representations are decoded, encoded, and aligned across individuals and datasets. It also emphasizes representational similarity with deep neural networks, hyperalignment, and links to behavioral performance in naturalistic settings.

keywords: the neural correlates of selective attention
topics.title: Spatial Attention and Visual Perception
topics.description: This cluster centers on how attention shapes visual processing, including selective and covert spatial attention, awareness, and perceptual learning. It also includes developmental and individual-difference effects such as pseudoneglect, bias in spatial attention, and training-related enhancement.

keywords: cortical depth profiles of mental imagery
multimodal neuroimaging case study of blindsense
topics.title: Visual Imagery and Topographic Perception
topics.description: This cluster centers on how the human visual system represents imagery, object perception, and completion processes across cortical areas. It also includes studies of blindness, blindsense, and multimodal neuroimaging evidence for topographic and spatiotemporal organization.
